name: header-navigation-menu
title: Header Navigation Menu
description: The Header's primary navigation, driven by a single `items` array (direct links or grouped panels) so one source of data renders two presentations: a floating NavigationMenu mega-menu from md up, and an inline accordion (native <details>) below md that expands each section in place and pushes the page down instead of floating over it. The whole nav collapses behind the HeaderMenuButton (aria-controls wired to the accordion by id). Drop it inside <Header> in place of HeaderNav when top-level destinations need grouped panels; outside a Header the accordion never collapses.
useWhen: A site header whose primary navigation needs mega-menu panels or grouped submenus, not just a flat row of links
Replacing HeaderNav inside a Header when top-level items open disclosure panels of grouped links
Multilingual headers with complex navigation (every label lives in the items array, so the header localizes per page)
avoidWhen: A header with a handful of flat top-level links (use HeaderNav + HeaderNavLink)
Navigation outside a page header (use NavigationMenu directly, or in-page / side navigation)
More than one primary navigation landmark per page

keyboard: From md up: NavigationMenu's disclosure-navigation contract — a panel opens on click, Enter, Space, or the orientation's arrow key (and hover-intent for pointers, never hover-only); Escape closes the open panel and returns focus to its trigger; Tab moves focus out of the bar

Below md: an inline accordion of native <details>/<summary> — Enter and Space toggle a section (browser-native), and sections share a name so only one is open at a time where the browser supports exclusive accordions

The whole mobile nav collapses behind the Header's menu button (a native button; Enter and Space toggle it), with aria-controls referencing the accordion landmark by id; collapsed content stays in the DOM (removed from the tab order while hidden, not hidden-while-focusable)
The current page carries aria-current=page with non-color indicators (a 2px block-end border and heavier weight on desktop, heavier weight in the accordion), never relying on color alone
notes: One items array renders both a NavigationMenu (md+) and a native <details> accordion (below md); only one is in the accessibility tree per breakpoint (the other is display:none), so a single Primary landmark is announced.

The mobile accordion's only added interactive surface is the native <details>/<summary>, which carries its own disclosure keyboard and screen-reader semantics; the desktop panels are NavigationMenu's, verified on that component.

The framework-agnostic path has no dedicated class: compose .cui-header + .cui-navigation-menu for the desktop bar and a <details> accordion for the mobile disclosure, wiring the menu button the same way the Header does.
